chunks.text: Now let's say I say there is a variable.

chunks.text: And one is the real part and one is the imaginary part.

chunks.title: Introduction to OOPs in Python
chunks.text: Real is equal to 6, img is equal to 8.

chunks.title: Introduction to OOPs in Python
chunks.text: I say that let's leave the real and imaginary part, I will give you a good example.

chunks.title: Introduction to OOPs in Python
chunks.text: Let's say a businessman has a sale of 6000 rupees.

chunks.title: Introduction to OOPs in Python
chunks.text: If it is less than rupees, then consider it as dollars.

chunks.title: Introduction to OOPs in Python
chunks.text: And the sale is of 6000 rupees.

chunks.title: Introduction to OOPs in Python
chunks.text: And its revenue or let's say its profit is of 2000 rupees.

chunks.title: Introduction to OOPs in Python
chunks.text: And let's say the same businessman is promoting, he is putting ads in billboards.

chunks.title: Introduction to OOPs in Python
chunks.text: Let's say he is hiring people, he is doing influencer marketing.

chunks.title: Introduction to OOPs in Python
chunks.text: Let's say his ad spend is 1000 rupees or dollars, whatever you consider.

chunks.title: Introduction to OOPs in Python
chunks.text: So his profit is 2000 rupees, ad spend is this much.

chunks.title: Introduction to OOPs in Python
chunks.text: And we can calculate its margin.

chunks.title: Introduction to OOPs in Python
chunks.text: So these are all things, we have stored in different variables.

chunks.title: Introduction to OOPs in Python
chunks.text: Now let's say I want to do this thing for another businessman.

chunks.title: Introduction to OOPs in Python
chunks.text: Now I will say that I can't make it in the same name.

chunks.title: Introduction to OOPs in Python
chunks.text: I will make it sale1 and profit1.

chunks.title: Introduction to OOPs in Python
chunks.text: I will make it ad1 and 2 to 2.

chunks.title: Introduction to OOPs in Python
chunks.text: And here the values of the other businessman will come.

chunks.title: Introduction to OOPs in Python
chunks.text: So if you say that I have to do this for a third businessman.

chunks.text: By businessman I mean businessman in case someone doesn't know Hindi well.

chunks.title: Introduction to OOPs in Python
chunks.text: So I have made it sales3, profit3, ad3.

chunks.title: Introduction to OOPs in Python
chunks.text: So I am maintaining the variables for 3 days.

chunks.text: I want to make a variable with the name of a businessman.

chunks.text: Or I would have done something where all the information of that businessman would have come in one place.

chunks.text: It would have been so good.

chunks.text: So classes and objects help us to do this.

chunks.text: I am telling you.

chunks.title: Introduction to OOPs in Python

chunks.title: Introduction to OOPs in Python
chunks.text: Finally, it goes to 0 and he dies.

chunks.text: So to track such variables, we make an entity called a player.

chunks.text: It would have been so good.

chunks.text: Now this thing will seem complicated to you.

chunks.title: Introduction to OOPs in Python
chunks.text: Is object oriented programming necessary?

chunks.title: Introduction to OOPs in Python
chunks.text: No.

chunks.title: Introduction to OOPs in Python
chunks.text: You can do procedural programming.

chunks.title: Introduction to OOPs in Python
chunks.text: You can make variables here.

chunks.title: Introduction to OOPs in Python
chunks.text: Object oriented programming, once you understand to a certain degree what it is,

chunks.title: Introduction to OOPs in Python
chunks.text: then it makes things easy for you.

chunks.title: Introduction to OOPs in Python
chunks.text: For example, just think that you have a main player.

chunks.title: Introduction to OOPs in Python
chunks.text: And there are a lot of people in the city who are enemies who will kill that player.

chunks.title: Introduction to OOPs in Python
chunks.text: So will you make variables again and again?

chunks.title: Introduction to OOPs in Python
chunks.text: No.

chunks.title: Introduction to OOPs in Python
chunks.text: You will not make different variables of the enemy and track their different properties.

chunks.title: Introduction to OOPs in Python
chunks.text: Then you will have to track the health of every enemy.

chunks.title: Introduction to OOPs in Python
chunks.text: Then you will have to track which weapons every enemy has.

chunks.title: Introduction to OOPs in Python
chunks.text: You will not want this.

chunks.title: Introduction to OOPs in Python
chunks.text: You would rather like to make an enemy class.

chunks.title: Introduction to OOPs in Python
chunks.text: Make a template so that the enemy gets everything.

chunks.title: Introduction to OOPs in Python
chunks.text: Along with his health, if he carries weapons,

chunks.title: Introduction to OOPs in Python
chunks.text: which weapons he is carrying,

chunks.title: Introduction to OOPs in Python
chunks.text: this data will get everything.

chunks.title: Introduction to OOPs in Python
chunks.text: So this is the idea of object oriented programming.

chunks.title: Introduction to OOPs in Python
chunks.text: So this is the idea of object oriented programming.

text: Object Oriented Programming is a very demanding thing which is used to make a lot of big companies and big products. Now what exactly is there in Object Oriented Programming that we can't do with our normal functional programming? Today I will tell you the whole story of Object Oriented Programming that you will understand what is Object Oriented Programming. You will never be in confusion that why are we doing the Object Oriented Programming that we are doing in this video? I will tell you everything from the basics to why we use it. Let's go to the computer screen and let's get started. Intro So to understand Object Oriented Programming properly we have to understand why we are introducing Object Oriented Programming. Why we will use Object Oriented Programming or what is the use of Object Oriented Programming. So first of all, you will see that whatever programming we have done till now is procedural programming. That is, we use functions. We use logic in our own program. Let's say we wrote a function by defining it as hello. And let's say this function printed hello. And then we called this function. No surprises there, hello will be printed. And I know you don't even want to see this thing, hello will be printed. Now let's say I say there is a variable. There is a variable and let's say there are two numbers. And one is the real part and one is the imaginary part. Real is equal to 6, img is equal to 8. I say that let's leave the real and imaginary part, I will give you a good example. Let's say a businessman has a sale of 6000 rupees. If it is less than rupees, then consider it as dollars. And the sale is of 6000 rupees. And its revenue or let's say its profit is of 2000 rupees. And let's say the same businessman is promoting, he is putting ads in billboards. Let's say he is hiring people, he is doing influencer marketing. Let's say his ad spend is 1000 rupees or dollars, whatever you consider. So his profit is 2000 rupees, ad spend is this much. And we can calculate its margin. So these are all things, we have stored in different variables. Now let's say I want to do this thing for another businessman. Now I will say that I can't make it in the same name. I will make it sale1 and profit1. I will make it ad1 and 2 to 2. And here the values of the other businessman will come. So if you say that I have to do this for a third businessman. By businessman I mean businessman in case someone doesn't know Hindi well. So I have made it sales3, profit3, ad3. So I am maintaining the variables for 3 days. I want to make a variable with the name of a businessman. Or I would have done something where all the information of that businessman would have come in one place. It would have been so good. So classes and objects help us to do this. Now you understand one more thing. If you are a gamer, then I will tell you. Suppose I have a city. Suppose GTA is a city and you must have played GTA 5. If you haven't played, then there is no problem. I am telling you. There is a player. That player roams around the whole city. He has a gun. His health is 0. So that player dies. The game is over. If his health is 100, it means that he can get beaten up a little. It happens like this. He can get beaten up a little. He can get beaten up with 2-4, 15-20 kicks. And he can take 2-3 bullets easily. Maybe he can eat more. So what happens? His health keeps decreasing. Finally, it goes to 0 and he dies. So to track such variables, we make an entity called a player. It would have been so good. We say that make a player. The health of the player will come automatically. What guns does the player have will come automatically. Along with this, there is a lot of information about the player. That will come. And we can also perform some operations on the player. Like we can say player.run. Now this thing will seem complicated to you. I am just giving you an idea. What we are doing is that the variables that were just numbers and data for us earlier, we are mapping it with the real world entity. We are saying that this is sales1, profit1, not add1. We will do this that Rajiv is a businessman. So we will say rajiv.sales. And suppose we have another businessman here. His name is Vikrant. Vikrant. We say Vikrant.sales. So I know that by doing .sales, the businessman's sale comes. So we do something like this that Vikrant is equal to new business. Something like this. And after doing this, we say that we have a new businessman. Now we can track his sales, revenue, profit, and all the things like ad spend. So this is the idea of object oriented programming. Is object oriented programming necessary? No. You can do procedural programming. You can make variables here. Object oriented programming, once you understand to a certain degree what it is, then it makes things easy for you. For example, just think that you have a main player. And there are a lot of people in the city who are enemies who will kill that player. So will you make variables again and again? No. You will not make different variables of the enemy and track their different properties. Then you will have to track the health of every enemy. Then you will have to track which weapons every enemy has. You will not want this. You would rather like to make an enemy class. Make a template so that the enemy gets everything. Along with his health, if he carries weapons, which weapons he is carrying, this data will get everything. So this is the idea of object oriented programming. So this is the idea of object oriented programming. So this is what you should know. The same thing can be done by creating variables. It can be done by spreading the mess. Spreading the mess means making a lot of variables like this. Make your program very big, but will it remain maintainable? No. Object oriented programming is a way with which you can understand the program easily and you can perform actions on it easily. Therefore, object oriented programming is a good way. that's why object oriented programming was made to make things easier now what is it, how classes and objects are made, I will tell you later but let's read a little about it there are two types of approaches in programming languages procedural programming and object oriented programming we were making functions, using logic from one function to another function, control was going on but what does object oriented programming do it maps all variables with real world concepts and entities now I will give you one more example suppose you have a railway form and you have three people one is Harry, one is Tom and one is Shubham Harry, Tom and Shubham, three people now what will happen here, the railway form all the information is pre filled if you have made a reservation you will know the form you have to write in it, which train you are going in train number, address, you pay money you get the ticket, so that type of form you get, through this form you get to know what all things you have to fill, so that if you write everything on a paper, then you might go you submit your form, the people sitting in front of you on the counter, they say that you have not filled the name you have not filled the age, you have not filled the train so that data is not missing, so you get a template you automatically get that template there are many forms there you get it easily, it is easy for you to fill what you have to give, to the person sitting on the ticket counter, this is easy for you so Harry, Tom and Shubham, these three have different forms and when they fill their data, then they will keep their own objects, their own forms so this railway form, we can consider it as a class what is this? it is a class class means a template, a blueprint which will explain how the objects made from this will look for example, this is a railway form, so railway form will be Harry's railway form, it will have Harry's information so I will write here, Harry's info form similarly, what is here?
